recordPolicy: This JSON file is the only machine-readable operational source for rule-remediation progress. Markdown receipts may explain completed slices, but they must not duplicate or define active rule rows, queue, blockers, or counts.

gateState.currentGate: Implementation-ready Layer 3 entry warrant exists for the selected helper-surface slice; stop before Layer 3 mutation.
gateState.mutationAuthorization: Layer 3 may mutate only the selected helper-surface slice after re-reading current state; no other rule/source/test/generated mutation is authorized by this gate.
gateState.nextLegalAction: Enter Layer 3 for one bounded slice that widens/polishes prohibit_runtime_helper_redeclarations for exact-equivalent public MapGen core helper redeclarations, then absorbs/deletes prohibit_foundation_duplicate_math_helper_redefinitions only to the covered extent.

findings.finding: Grit conversion falsified on 2026-07-01: the predicate is static import/export source shape, but current Habitat/Grit source execution obeys .gritignore, which excludes **/test/ and **/*.test.ts. A temporary forbidden deep import in mods/mod-swooper-maps/test/ecology/classify-biomes.test.ts was ignored by Grit, while the existing Habitat script owns the actual test-file corpus. Do not mark this row no-action via Grit until Habitat has an explicit test-file scan capability or the authority is split into non-test public-surface rails plus a separate test policy.
findings.handling: Restored current Habitat script runner; row remains packetNeeded split-by-owner and sealed blocker. No package tests added.

findings.finding: The row did not require a user semantic gate or a new recipe-step positive authority. Canonical Morphology docs already ban overlays as Physics inputs; the existing standard-recipe Morphology context is the honest owner for this static source rail.

findings.finding: `bun habitat fix --dry-run` is not a clean remediation for the red Biome-backed hygiene gate. It plans broad historical/docs pattern rewrites and reports Markdown parse errors, rather than a bounded formatting/import hygiene mutation.
findings.handling: Do not run `bun habitat fix` as the Layer 3 remediation for this row. The row remains a runtime/source residual stop gate until workspace hygiene is handled as its own cleanup or the rule is routed to a narrower native Biome fix path.

The canonical JSON itself is the durable operational record; Domino 92 is the narrative receipt.
